body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` **Triggered by:** commit 685f2f019a (PR #58396) by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #58396 added several new public APIs to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (released as `1.0.0-beta.23`): - New **`PlatformHeaders`** static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) - New **`RequestIdMiddleware`** that sets the `x-request-id` response header on every HTTP response (resolved in priority order: OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID) - New **`RequestIdBaggagePropagator`** for end-to-end distributed tracing correlation - **Breaking change**: Replaced 6 individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId()`, `AddAgentServerVersion()`, `AddAgentServerLogging()`, `UseAgentServerRequestId()`, `UseAgentServerVersion()`, `UseAgentServerLogging()`) with a single `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair - **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` — users must now use `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` ## Gaps Found - `PlatformHeaders` static class is a new public API that forms the wire contract between Foundry platform, agent containers, and downstream storage — it is not mentioned anywhere in the README - `RequestIdMiddleware` behavior (`x-request-id` header resolution logic) is not documented in Key Concepts or Examples - The migration from `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName/ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` to `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey/ChatIsolationKey` is not reflected in the README - No code example showing how to use `PlatformHeaders` constants in a custom middleware or handler
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` In the **Key Concepts** section, add a new subsection for `PlatformHeaders` after the `Health endpoint` entry: ```markdown ### PlatformHeaders A static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants used across the AgentServer wire protocol. Use these constants instead of hard-coding header name strings: | Constant | Header name | Direction | |---|---|---| | `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request + Response | | `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response | | `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response | | `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request | | `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request | | `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request | | `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request | The `x-request-id` response header is set automatically on every response by `RequestIdMiddleware` (registered by `AgentHostBuilder` and by `AddAgentServerCore()`). The value is resolved in priority order: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → new GUID. ``` Also update the **Logging** entry under **Troubleshooting** to mention the migration from the old extension methods: Replace the existing sentence: > for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it. With: > for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it. Note: if upgrading from beta.22, the six individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId()`, `AddAgentServerVersion()`, `AddAgentServerLogging()`, and their `Use*` counterparts) have been consolidated into this single pair. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add a new snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage in a test file at: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample_PlatformHeaders.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples { public class Sample_PlatformHeaders { [Test] public void UsePlatformHeaderConstants() { #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders // Use PlatformHeaders constants instead of hard-coding header name strings. string requestIdHeader = PlatformHeaders.RequestId; // "x-request-id" string serverVersionHeader = PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion; // "x-platform-server" string userIsolationKey = PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey; // "x-agent-user-isolation-key" string chatIsolationKey = PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey; // "x-agent-chat-isolation-key" #endregion #if !SNIPPET Assert.That(requestIdHeader, Is.EqualTo("x-request-id")); Assert.That(serverVersionHeader, Is.EqualTo("x-platform-server")); Assert.That(userIsolationKey, Is.EqualTo("x-agent-user-isolation-key")); Assert.That(chatIsolationKey, Is.EqualTo("x-agent-chat-isolation-key")); #endif } } } ``` Add the following snippet placeholder to the README in the **Examples** section (currently it only has a link to samples — add a subsection before the link): ```markdown ### Reading platform header names Use `PlatformHeaders` constants to access header names without hard-coding strings: ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already contains all required sections. The gaps are within existing sections: 1. **Key concepts** — missing `PlatformHeaders` subsection (add as described above) 2. **Examples** — only has a bare link; should include at least one inline snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage 3. **Troubleshooting / Logging** — should note the beta.22 → beta.23 extension method consolidation Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for section formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders class and RequestIdMiddleware behavior in Core README
